Sidebar Items

Sidebar Items

Section titled “Sidebar Items”

Type of sidebar

Section titled “Type of sidebar”
  • Doc: link to a doc page, associating it with the sidebar
  • Link: link to any internal or external page
  • HTML: renders pure HTML in the item’s position
  • Category: creates a dropdown of sidebar items
  • Autogenerated: generate a sidebar slice automatically
  • Ref: link to a doc page, without making the item take part in navigation generation

Doc: link to a doc

Section titled “Doc: link to a doc”

Use the doc type to link to a doc page and assign that doc to a sidebar:

Example

Section titled “Example”

An item with type doc can be simply a string representing its ID:

  • Longhand
  • Shorthand

If you use the doc shorthand or autogenerated sidebar you would lose the ability to customize the sidebar label through item definition.

You can, however, use the sidebar_label Markdown front matter within that doc, which has higher precedence over the label key in the sidebar item. Similarly, you can use sidebar_custom_props to declare custom metadata for a doc page.

Link: link to any page

Section titled “Link: link to any page”

Use the link type to link to any page (internal or external) that is not a doc.

Example:

HTML: render custom markup

Section titled “HTML: render custom markup”

Use the html type to render custom HTML sidebar within the item’s <li> tag.

Note

html value will be rendered inside li tag

This can be useful for inserting custom items such as dividers, section titles, ads, and images.

Example:

Category: create a hierarchy

Section titled “Category: create a hierarchy”

Use the category type to create a hierarchy ( expandable group ) of sidebar items.

Example

Section titled “Example”

A category item can be represented by an object whose key is its label , and the value is an array of subitems .

  • Longhand
  • Shorthand
  • Longhand
  • Simplified
  • Further Simplified

Note how the two consecutive category ( in Further Simplified tab ) shorthands are compressed into one object with two entries .

This syntax generates a sidebar slice :

you shouldn’t see that object as one bulk item—this object is unwrapped, with each entry becoming a separate item, and they spliced together with the rest of the items to form the final sidebar level.

Sidebar slices are also important when discussing autogenerated sidebars.

You can also omit that enclosing array wherever you have an array of items that is reduced to one category shorthand .

  • Before
  • After

Category links

Section titled “Category links”

With category links, clicking on a category can navigate you to another page .

Generated index page

Section titled “Generated index page”

You can auto-generate an index page that displays all the direct children of this category. The slug allows you to customize the generated page’s route, which defaults to /category/[categoryName].

Example

result :

Doc link

Section titled “Doc link”

A category can link to an existing document.

Embedding generated index in doc page

Section titled “Embedding generated index in doc page”

You can embed the generated cards list in a normal doc page as well, as long as the doc is used as a category index page .

To do so, you need to use the DocCardList component, paired with the useCurrentSidebarCategory hook.

  • Example
  • Result

Collapsible categories

Section titled “Collapsible categories”

We support the option to expand/collapse categories. Categories are collapsible by default, but you can disable collapsing with collapsible: false.

To make all categories non-collapsible by default, set the sidebarCollapsible option in plugin-content-docs to false:

Note

The option in sidebars.js takes precedence over plugin configuration

Expanded categories by default

Section titled “Expanded categories by default”

Collapsible categories are collapsed by default. If you want them to be expanded on the first render, you can set collapsed to false:

global config


Sources

Section titled “Sources”
  • https://docusaurus.io/docs/sidebar/items
